pi_items.source: Verified against task setup scripts and environment structure.
Verified via install_gvsig.sh which downloads Natural Earth 110m physical data.
Verified via install_gvsig.sh which downloads Natural Earth 110m cultural data.
Calculated exact total bounds of Brazil using geopandas on the downloaded ne_110m_admin_0_countries.shp file.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real public-domain shapefiles from Natural Earth 110m. The input file paths and expected output path are verified to be correct. The provided brazil_bbox in the metadata is a rough approximation; the actual bounding box for Brazil in the dataset is exactly [-73.987, -33.768, -34.730, 5.244]. A critical flaw exists in the verifier's extent logic: the 110m rivers dataset contains only 13 global river features, and clipping them to Brazil results in exactly 2 features with a bounding box center X of roughly -60.22. Because the verifier expects the center X of the output clipped rivers to be greater than -60 (mistakenly assuming it will match Brazil's geographic center of -54), a perfectly executed task will fail the 'spatial extent' check.
